title: End-to-end validation of Windows integration
description: Validate the full setup: GlazeWM auto-launches on login; Kitty opens on Alt+Enter and receives keyboard focus correctly on Alt+Tab return; hjkl focus/move bindings work; chezmoi apply from Windows PowerShell re-applies GlazeWM config cleanly; chezmoi apply from WSL2 leaves Windows paths untouched. Document any friction points.

title: Add run_once install script for GlazeWM on Windows
description: Add home/run_once_install-glazewm.ps1.tmpl to the Chezmoi source. Script should: guard with `{{ if ne .chezmoi.os "windows" }}{{ return }}{{ end }}`, run `winget install glzr-io.glazewm --silent --accept-package-agreements`, and be idempotent (winget install is safe to re-run). Validate from the Windows-side Chezmoi mirror: `chezmoi apply --dry-run` to preview, then apply. Commit and push.

title: Set up Windows-side Chezmoi mirror
description: Install Chezmoi on Windows (`winget install twpayne.chezmoi`). Init it pointing at the WSL2 source: `chezmoi init --source '\\wsl$\Ubuntu\home\hactar\.local\share\chezmoi'` from PowerShell. Run `chezmoi apply` and verify dot_glzr lands at %USERPROFILE%\.glzr\glazewm\config.yaml. Document the two-apply workflow (WSL apply for Linux side, PowerShell apply for Windows side).

title: Add GlazeWM config to Chezmoi source tree
description: Add the validated ~/.glzr/glazewm/config.yaml to the Chezmoi source as home/dot_glzr/glazewm/config.yaml. Run `chezmoi add ~/.glzr/glazewm/config.yaml` or manually place in source tree. Commit and push. Verify `chezmoi apply` from WSL2 leaves Linux $HOME unaffected (relies on .chezmoiignore from Chezmoi-c0i.4).

title: Add OS-conditional .chezmoiignore for Windows/Linux split
description: Template home/.chezmoiignore to ignore Windows-specific paths (dot_glzr, AppData) on Linux, and Linux-specific paths (.config/nvim, .local, .zshrc, etc.) on Windows. This is the foundational gate that allows a single Chezmoi repo to serve both sides without cross-contamination. Apply from WSL2 and verify chezmoi status is clean.

title: Configure GlazeWM to launch on Windows startup
description: Add GlazeWM to Windows startup via the startup folder (shell:startup shortcut) or the tray menu 'Launch on startup' toggle if present in the installed version. Validate by signing out and back in. Only do this after the config is stable—don't auto-launch a broken config.

title: Write minimal GlazeWM config
description: Author ~/.glzr/glazewm/config.yaml with: i3-style hjkl focus/move bindings, Alt+Enter to launch Kitty from WSL2, workspace switching (Alt+1-9), Alt+Shift+Space to toggle floating, Alt+Shift+Q to close, Alt+Shift+C to reload config. Iterate until Kitty launches correctly and focus/window-movement bindings work.

title: Install GlazeWM via winget
description: Run `winget install glzr-io.glazewm` from a Windows PowerShell session. Verify GlazeWM launches, attaches to the desktop, and the tray icon appears. No config needed at this stage.

title: Add Windows integration: GlazeWM + Chezmoi mirror
description: Set up GlazeWM as a tiling WM on Windows to fix Kitty ergonomics (launch hotkey, Alt+Tab focus, window management), and establish Chezmoi management of Windows-side dotfiles with a Windows-native Chezmoi mirror pointing at the WSL2 source repo.
